Summary: Migrate existing developer and asset LOI records into the tracker
Description:
Hierarchy / linkage
- Issue: TGTM-1052 — Migrate existing developer and asset LOI records into the tracker
- Issue Type: Sub-task
- Workstream: LOI pipeline management
- Parent: TGTM-134 — Create LOI tracker (Story)
- Grandparent / Epic Context: TGTM-12 — Implement sales, CRM, ABM, and partnership operations

Original intent
This Sub-task completes a focused implementation step under its parent item. Original description: Collect any current LOI information and enter it into the new tracker so signed and pending LOIs are captured in one place.

How we are doing this
- Confirm the parent objective and the exact record, process, or artifact this item must improve.
- Create the work product using the agreed CRM fields, stage model, and ownership rules.
- Validate the output with representative records or examples.
- Document completion evidence and handoff instructions for the next owner.

Preliminary solution
Use a dedicated LOI tracker inside the CRM or operating workspace, linked to developer, asset, partner, and opportunity records. Configure statuses such as Draft, Sent, Under Review, Negotiation, Signed, Expired, and Not Proceeding; require document links, expiry dates, owner, and next action before a record can be considered current.

CRM / operating data model
Use this item to strengthen the LOI pipeline management layer of the GTM operating system. Minimum fields or data points to capture: LOI status, counterparty type, asset / developer name, signed date, expiry / validity date, commercial notes, document link. Where possible, link records back to the relevant developer, investor, partner, opportunity, LOI, referral source, campaign, or meeting record so management reporting can connect activity to pipeline outcomes.

Expected deliverable
A populated tracker with required fields, ownership rules, views, and update cadence defined.

Dependencies / inputs
- existing LOI records and document links
- developer / asset opportunity data
- legal or commercial owner input
- developer target list
- asset onboarding criteria
- sales stage definitions

Implementation notes
- Keep the workflow lightweight enough for founder-led execution but structured enough to support SDR handoff, weekly reporting, and scale-up.
- Use clear naming conventions and required fields so records can be filtered, audited, and reviewed without manual reconstruction.
- Avoid creating disconnected spreadsheets when the same information should live in the CRM source of truth.
- Where automation is not available yet, create a manual operating process with owner, cadence, and review trigger.

Risks / watchouts
- over-customizing the CRM before process-market fit is proven
- incomplete records causing unreliable reporting
- unclear ownership leading to stale pipeline data

Acceptance criteria
- Tracker contains the required fields, statuses, owners, and next-action structure.
- Initial records are loaded or sample records demonstrate the structure.
- Team members can filter the tracker for priority, status, owner, and next step.

Summary: Review and package SDR objection-handling snippets into a reusable playbook
Description:
Hierarchy / linkage
- Issue: TGTM-1042 — Review and package SDR objection-handling snippets into a reusable playbook
- Issue Type: Sub-task
- Workstream: SDR enablement and objection handling
- Parent: TGTM-132 — Create SDR objection-handling snippets (Story)
- Grandparent / Epic Context: TGTM-12 — Implement sales, CRM, ABM, and partnership operations

Original intent
This Sub-task completes a focused implementation step under its parent item. Original description: Consolidate the approved responses into a standardized SDR-ready format with guidance on when to use each snippet and any follow-up questions.

How we are doing this
- Review the parent workflow, target audience, and existing messaging before writing the asset.
- Draft concise working copy with personalization points, proof points, CTA, and objection coverage.
- Package the copy in a format that can be pasted into CRM sequences, call notes, playbooks, or outreach templates.
- Review for accuracy, tone, compliance sensitivity, and practical usability by SDRs or founders.

Preliminary solution
Convert the objection category into short, approved SDR-ready responses with context, recommended talk track, supporting proof point, disqualifier language, and escalation path. Keep responses modular so they can be reused in calls, email replies, LinkedIn follow-ups, and founder conversations.

CRM / operating data model
Use this item to strengthen the SDR enablement and objection handling layer of the GTM operating system. Minimum fields or data points to capture: ICP, objection category, approved response, proof point, fallback CTA, escalation path. Where possible, link records back to the relevant developer, investor, partner, opportunity, LOI, referral source, campaign, or meeting record so management reporting can connect activity to pipeline outcomes.

Expected deliverable
A recurring operating ritual with agenda, data inputs, decision log, and follow-up action tracker.

Dependencies / inputs
- objection-handling playbook
- compliance guidance
- approved proof points

Implementation notes
- Keep the workflow lightweight enough for founder-led execution but structured enough to support SDR handoff, weekly reporting, and scale-up.
- Use clear naming conventions and required fields so records can be filtered, audited, and reviewed without manual reconstruction.
- Avoid creating disconnected spreadsheets when the same information should live in the CRM source of truth.
- Where automation is not available yet, create a manual operating process with owner, cadence, and review trigger.

Risks / watchouts
- unclear scope
- lack of owner accountability
- output not connected to the parent operating workflow

Acceptance criteria
- Copy is written in an approved founder/SDR voice and tied to the correct audience and parent workflow.
- Personalization fields, CTA, proof points, and follow-up logic are clearly specified.
- The asset is ready to paste into CRM, sequence tooling, or the relevant playbook without further structural work.
